H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: The largest HEALING entry (n=95) shows mean 7.15 (std 1.78) with strong confidence and 66 % positive ratings. Qualitative data emphasize noticeable improvement for drivers and listeners, with balanced rhythm and high music liking. YouTube reach is highest in the dataset (30 views) but lacks retention detail. AutoGluon predicts 6.86, modestly lower than observed, suggesting the protocol may exceed model expectations.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Very effective for overstimulation; listeners often feel settled within ten to twenty minutes.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: n=51, mean 6.92 (std 1.73), strong confidence, 65 % positive. Listeners report noticeable improvement; music opinions are split between liked and neutral. YouTube views=5. AutoGluon prediction (6.86) aligns closely, reinforcing its efficacy despite lower reach than L1.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Good for calming overload, though music reception varies among users.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: n=47, mean 7.04 (std 1.68), meaningful confidence, 64 % positive. Feedback highlights use before sleep with moderate settle times (5‑10 minutes). YouTube views=8. AutoGluon prediction matches observed mean, indicating reliable performance across overstimulation descent.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Helps especially before sleep; most notice a positive shift after a few minutes.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: The protocol recorded n=67 responses with mean 7.22 (std 1.93) and strong confidence. Distribution skews toward high ratings (7‑10) and 63 % positive endorsement. Qualitative notes indicate most listeners (≈83 %) are self‑listeners, reporting moderate to noticeable improvement with a typical settle time of about 10 minutes and balanced rhythm perception. YouTube data show only 7 views, limiting retention insight. AutoGluon predicts a score of 7.84, closely matching the observed mean, reinforcing its efficacy signal.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Most people felt anxiety ease within about ten minutes and liked the balanced rhythm.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: With n=48, mean 6.94 (std 1.62) and meaningful confidence, this protocol displays a moderately high positive rate (67 %). Listener comments reveal a split in music opinion and faster settle times (~5 minutes). No YouTube retention data (6 views). AutoGluon importance ranking places hz_abs near the top, but predicted scores align with observed performance, suggesting stability across groove variations.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: Helpful for many, though some felt only a slight shift and had mixed feelings about the music.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: The protocol has n=36, mean 7.08 (std 1.89) and meaningful confidence; 61 % of ratings are positive. Qualitative feedback highlights noticeable improvement for most, with settle times often extending to the session end. YouTube reach is minimal (4 views). AutoGluon does not prioritize this level, implying L1 may be optimal for this anxiety descent tier.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Generally calming, but a few listeners needed the full session to notice improvement.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: Crisis protocol n=43, mean 6.35 (std 1.70), meaningful confidence, and 53 % positive endorsement. Despite lower mean, 33 % of respondents report significant improvement, reflecting the expected low baseline in acute distress. YouTube views are 6, with no retention metrics. AutoGluon ranks H-CR-L2 higher, flagging a potential routing refinement for H-CR-L1.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Provides relief during tough moments, with many reporting quick improvement.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: n=33, mean 6.52 (std 1.70), meaningful confidence, 55 % positive. Listener remarks are mixed, with equal numbers loving or being neutral toward the music. YouTube views=4. AutoGluon predicts this as the top crisis protocol (score 7.96), creating a notable prediction‑observed gap that suggests a possible entrainment timing mismatch.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Some find strong calm, others are neutral; overall it helps but not dramatically.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: n=27, mean 6.52 (std 1.91), meaningful confidence, 52 % positive. Rapid settle times (immediate to 5 minutes) are reported by a majority, though some note a “busy” rhythm. YouTube views=5. AutoGluon prediction aligns with observed mean, indicating consistent performance within the crisis zone.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Settles fast for many, though a few think the rhythm feels a bit busy.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: Sleep protocol n=54, mean 7.19 (std 1.86), strong confidence, 65 % positive. Self‑listeners dominate responses, reporting moderate to significant improvement and rapid settle (<10 minutes). YouTube views=4. AutoGluon selects this as top sleep protocol (pred 7.54), confirming strong alignment.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Strong sleep aid; most users feel relaxed quickly and enjoy the rhythm.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: n=45, mean 7.09 (std 1.55), meaningful confidence, 67 % positive. Mixed listener types (patients, supporters) note balanced rhythm but occasional “busy” perception. YouTube views=5. AutoGluon does not rank it as top, suggesting L1 may have slight advantage.
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Works well for many trying to fall asleep, though some find the rhythm a little busy.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: n=28, mean 7.07 (std 1.68), meaningful confidence, 64 % positive. Listeners report noticeable improvement, though some did not settle. YouTube views=3. AutoGluon prediction aligns with observed mean, indicating stable but modest performance.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Generally helps relax, but not everyone settles right away.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: Focus & Clarity hold protocol n=41, mean 6.46 (std 1.82), meaningful confidence, 56 % positive. Several respondents describe a “slightly busy” rhythm, and settle times vary from immediate to 5 minutes. YouTube views=5. AutoGluon predicts a higher top‑rank protocol (T-FC-L3), suggesting a possible benefit from extending the hold phase for L1.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Aids focus, but some find the rhythm slightly distracting.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: n=32, mean 7.19 (std 1.60), meaningful confidence, 66 % positive. Listeners report significant improvement; rhythm perceived as balanced. YouTube views=4. AutoGluon ranks this below L3, indicating room for optimization.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Improves focus for many; rhythm feels balanced for most listeners.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: n=35, mean 7.63 (std 1.97), meaningful confidence, 71 % positive. This is the flagship focus protocol, with strong qualitative reports of significant improvement and balanced rhythm. YouTube views=15, the series’ highest. AutoGluon predicts 7.73, closely matching observed efficacy.
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: Top focus tool; most users report a strong boost and like the beat.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: Creative‑Flow hold protocol n=34, mean 6.74 (std 1.73), meaningful confidence, 53 % positive. Qualitative comments show slight to moderate improvement with immediate settle for many. YouTube views=2. AutoGluon assigns modest importance to view metrics; predicted score matches observed mean.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Supports creative flow, but many report only a mild lift.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: n=24, mean 6.79 (std 1.41), meaningful confidence, 58 % positive. Listeners note moderate to significant improvement and settle around 10 minutes. YouTube views=2. AutoGluon does not flag this as a standout, aligning with its modest mean.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Liked by those who try it; offers a modest boost to creativity.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: n=18, mean 7.28 (std 1.32), developing confidence, 67 % positive. Highest mean among Creative‑Flow variants; listeners report notable improvement and mixed usage (sleep, work). YouTube views=7, the highest in this sub‑group. AutoGluon predicts a strong score, confirming its superior performance.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: One of the better creative‑flow experiences, with noticeable improvement for many.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: Maintenance & Balance hold protocol n=25, mean 7.04 (std 1.34), meaningful confidence, 64 % positive. Qualitative data show longer settle times (up to 20 minutes) for many. YouTube views=3. AutoGluon predicts the L3 variant as top, hinting at potential benefit from adjusting arc length.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Balances mood steadily, though it can take longer to feel the effect.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: n=22, mean 6.68 (std 2.03), meaningful confidence, 59 % positive. High variance and mixed rhythm feedback (“slightly busy”) are reported. YouTube views=4. AutoGluon does not prioritize this level, suggesting refinement may be needed.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Mixed results; some enjoy the balance, others find the rhythm off.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: n=14, mean 6.93 (std 1.73), developing confidence, 71 % positive. Listeners note good balance but some fail to settle. YouTube views=3. AutoGluon predicts it as top maintenance protocol (7.69), indicating a promising direction despite limited data.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Promising balance aid, but a few listeners did not settle fully.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: Mood & Energy ascent protocol n=39, mean 6.74 (std 1.94), meaningful confidence, 54 % positive. AutoGluon predicts a markedly higher score (8.23), creating a large prediction‑observed gap. YouTube views=8; qualitative data show delayed settle for a subset, hinting at mismatched entrainment.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Intended to lift mood, but many experience only moderate change and need time to settle.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: n=25, mean 6.76 (std 1.71), meaningful confidence, 64 % positive. Listeners generally like the music; settle times vary. YouTube views=8. AutoGluon cross‑bucket suggests applicability to crisis and maintenance zones, meriting further testing.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Good for low‑energy days; rhythm is generally liked and effects are steady.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: n=18, mean 6.28 (std 1.74), developing confidence, 56 % positive. Lower mean and high drift indicate instability; some listeners report no effect. YouTube views=4. AutoGluon does not flag this level, reinforcing its weaker status.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Less effective than earlier levels; several listeners report no noticeable change.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: Motivation & Drive ascent protocol n=22, mean 6.36 (std 2.01), meaningful confidence, 45 % positive. Qualitative notes reveal early settling for many but a large prediction‑observed gap (AutoGluon 7.88). This discrepancy signals possible entrainment timing issues.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Aims to boost drive but outcomes are mixed and less than expected.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: n=14, mean 6.21 (std 1.63), developing confidence, 43 % positive. Listeners report slower settle times (often >20 minutes) and mixed rhythm perception. YouTube views=7. AutoGluon predicts lower than observed, suggesting modest efficacy.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Provides some motivation, but many need time to feel any benefit.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: n=13, mean 7.15 (std 2.38), developing confidence, 62 % positive. Highest mean in motivation series but with high variance. YouTube views=7. AutoGluon does not prioritize this level, indicating a need to stabilize the ascent arc.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Strong motivation scores for some, yet reactions differ considerably.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: Mind & Clarity descent protocol n=24, mean 6.75 (std 1.62), meaningful confidence, 54 % positive. Two respondents reported worsening condition. YouTube views=8. AutoGluon predicts X-MC-L2 as top, suggesting modest room for improvement.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Clears mental fog for many, though a couple felt worse after listening.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: n=16, mean 6.81 (std 1.64), developing confidence, 56 % positive. Qualitative feedback shows significant improvements for many. YouTube views=12. AutoGluon predicts a high score (7.54), exceeding observed mean, flagging a potential under‑delivery.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Generally helpful for clarity; most listeners notice a modest improvement.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: n=10, mean 5.70 (std 2.45), developing confidence, 50 % positive. Distribution is bimodal with high variance, indicating subgroup effects. YouTube views=8. AutoGluon provides no strong prediction, underscoring its status as an outlier requiring redesign.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Results vary widely; some find it helpful, others see little effect or even a drop.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The data indicate stable efficacy for HEALING, strong peaks for THRIVING, and higher variance for TRANSFORMING.
• HEALING shows the largest sample (n=574) with low variance, matching its calming design.
• THRIVING delivers the highest observed means, especially in Focus & Clarity, but with mixed spread.
• TRANSFORMING protocols exhibit the widest spread and lower averages, consistent with ascending‑arc intent.
• YouTube reach is uniformly low, hinting at under‑discovery across series.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
Series‑level patterns align with their design intent while revealing nuanced listener responses.
• HEALING protocols cluster around 6.9‑7.2 mean, with anxiety and overstimulation tiers performing most consistently.
• THRIVING shows differentiated performance; Focus & Clarity (T-FC-L3) tops the series, whereas Creative Flow is modest.
• TRANSFORMING carries the greatest variance (std≈1.9) and lower means, especially in Motivation where observed scores lag behind AutoGluon predictions.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
A few protocols diverge sharply between observed outcomes and model expectations.
• X-ME-L1 exhibits a large prediction‑observed gap (pred 8.23 vs mean 6.74), suggesting possible population mismatch or entrainment timing issues.
• H-CR-L2 is AutoGluon‑ranked as top crisis protocol yet records only a modest mean (6.52) and modest positive endorsement.
• X-MC-L3 records the lowest mean (5.70) with high variance, indicating a bimodal response that may reflect subgroup effects.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Targeted refinements could improve efficacy and discoverability.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Increase library discoverability (e.g., SEO, cross‑promotion) – all series suffer from low YouTube views.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Investigate bimodal outcomes for X-MC-L3 and consider a L2 variant to address subgroup divergence.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Extend hold‑phase duration for T-FC-L1 to mitigate “slightly busy” rhythm feedback and raise mean scores.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] Refine ASCENT arc parameters for motivation protocols (X-MD-L1/L2/L3) to align observed means with high AutoGluon predictions.
• [SINGLE SOURCE] Adjust Adviser routing for H-CR-L1 to prioritize crisis listeners, potentially boosting its modest mean.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
Embedding drift is prevalent, yet semantic clustering remains underpowered.
• CROSS‑RUN DRIFT: 28 of 30 protocols show similarity < 0.85, indicating substantial commentary evolution.
• CROSS‑PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: Insufficient protocol embeddings to form reliable clusters.
• Confidence in drift signals is moderate; further embedding collection is recommended.
